perturbation.original_text: All Confidential Information remains the property of Disclosure and/or its licensors.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information is subject to standard intellectual property protection.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the explicit statement of ownership with the vague reference to 'standard intellectual property protection' creates a contradiction. The original clause clearly states ownership rests with the Discloser and/or its licensors. The changed clause does not specify ownership, but implies protection under general intellectual property laws. This becomes contradictory if 'standard intellectual property protection' is interpreted differently than the original statement of ownership and rights belonging to 'Disclosure and/or its licensors,' which could cause disputes in the future.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership: All Confidential Information remains the property of Disclosure and/or its licensors.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states that 'All Confidential Information remains the property of Disclosure and/or its licensors.' The changed text replaces this explicit ownership statement with a vague reference to 'standard intellectual property protection.' The Contradicted Text also states that 'All Confidential Information remains the property of Disclosure and/or its licensors.' Because the Changed Text doesn't explicitly mention that the Confidential Information is owned by 'Disclosure and/or its licensors' and instead vaguely refers to 'standard intellectual property protection' it creates a direct contradiction to the Contradicted text. This aligns with the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' as it removes essential language regarding ownership creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to its employees and information which may be retained in non-tangible form by persons who have had access to the Confidential Information, including without limitation general ideas, concepts, know-how provided that prior agreement with such parties sufficient to require that party to treat the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to its employees.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'provided that prior agreement with such parties sufficient to require that party to treat the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement', the text no longer specifies the requirement for a prior agreement ensuring confidentiality when disclosing confidential information to its employees. This contradicts the overall intent of the agreement by removing the obligation to ensure confidentiality from employees with access to the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Obligation of Confidentiality. For a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, Recipient agrees to use the same care and discretion to avoid disclosure, publication, or dissemination of Discloser's Confidential Information as it uses with its own similar information that it does not wish to disclose publish, or disseminate, but in no event shall Recipient use less than reasonable care to protect Discloser's Confidential Information. Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to its employees and information which may be retained in non-tangible form by persons who have had access to the Confidential Information, including without limitation general ideas, concepts, know-how provided that prior agreement with such parties sufficient to require that party to treat the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for a prior agreement to ensure confidentiality when disclosing confidential information to employees. The contradicted text states the Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to its employees 'provided that prior agreement with such parties sufficient to require that party to treat the Confidential Information in accordance with this Agreement.' Therefore, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text by removing this requirement, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In any legal proceeding arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to an award of its costs and reasonable attorneys' fees. The parties agree that Utah state and federal courts shall have jurisdiction and venue in any such proceeding.
perturbation.changed_text: In any legal proceeding arising out of this Agreement, the parties agree that Utah state and federal courts shall have jurisdiction and venue in any such proceeding.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'the prevailing party shall be entitled to an award of its costs and reasonable attorneys' fees' creates an in-text contradiction because it removes a crucial clause regarding cost allocation in legal disputes. This contradicts the intention of deterring frivolous lawsuits and fairly compensating the winning party for legal expenses. Without this clause, the agreement lacks clarity on who bears the financial burden of litigation, potentially leading to disagreements and undermining the contract's enforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General. a. This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of Utah (without regard to conflicts of laws provisions). In any legal proceeding arising out of this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to an award of its costs and reasonable attorneys' fees. The parties agree that Utah state and federal courts shall have jurisdiction and venue in any such proceeding.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that in any legal proceeding, the prevailing party is entitled to costs and attorney fees. The changed text omits this provision. The contradicted text includes the original, unedited version of the clause, which includes the attorney fees provision. Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text, as one includes a provision that the other removes, leading to a conflict regarding the allocation of legal costs.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient certifies that the Confidential Information will only be used for the purposes expressly stated herein and will not be rented, leased, sold, sublicensed, assigned, or otherwise transferred.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient will make reasonable efforts to ensure the Confidential Information is not rented, leased, sold, sublicensed, assigned, or otherwise transferred.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the certification that the confidential information 'will only be used for the purposes expressly stated herein' and replacing it with 'Recipient will make reasonable efforts to ensure...' turns a certain obligation into one that is based on effort. This contradicts the intended strict control and restriction of the information's use. If another section claims that the Recipient is responsible for any form of leaked information, this section will create a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General. b. Export Constraints. Recipient certifies that the Confidential Information will only be used for the purposes expressly stated herein and will not be rented, leased, sold, sublicensed, assigned, or otherwise transferred. Recipient shall adhere to the U.S. Export Administration laws and regulations and shall not export or re-export any technical data or products received from Discloser or the direct product of such technical data to any proscribed country listed in the U.S. Export Administration regulations unless properly authorized by the U.S. government.
perturbation.justification: The original text certified that the Confidential Information 'will only be used for the purposes expressly stated herein'. The changed text weakens this commitment to 'Recipient will make reasonable efforts to ensure...'. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text relies on the original strict certification, and the changed text replaces that strict certification with a 'reasonable efforts' clause. The original text imposes a strict obligation, while the changed text suggests a more flexible, effort-based standard, leading to a direct conflict and inconsistency regarding the level of commitment expected from the Recipient.
